actorRef.catalogSelectHelp: Choose an actor from the list. If the right actor is not listed, you can create new actors in the "Actors" tool. Created actors will appear in this picker.

wizard.stepAccess: Information about use of the object
wizard.reviewHint: Fix any issues in the sections, then save.

validation.identificationRequired: Enter a title or an object number.
validation.titleMaxLength: Title must be 200 characters or less
validation.objectNumberMaxLength: Object identification number must be 200 characters or less

recordImages.heading: Images
recordImages.hintCreate: Use Add file, set role and context, pick an image, then Move file to the save queue. Images upload after the record is created.
recordImages.hintEdit: Uploaded images show file details from the server. Queue more files the same way; they upload when you save the record.
recordImages.ownerOnly: Only the collection owner can add or remove images.
recordImages.draftNoFileHint: Choose an image file before saving to the queue.
recordImages.pendingTitle: Queued for upload when you save
recordImages.pendingHint: Added images stay in this queue until you save the record. After saving, they appear in the record’s image list.
recordImages.deleteConfirm: Remove this image from the record? The file will be deleted.
recordImages.uploadError: The record was saved but one or more images could not be uploaded. You can try again from this page.
recordImages.galleryOpenFullSize: Open full size in new tab

saveItemLabels.title: title
saveItemLabels.objectHistoryEventTimeEntry: time for the event related to the object
saveItemLabels.objectHistoryEventPlace: place for the event related to the object

temporal.dateQualifierHelp: How the object-related point in time sits within a temporal bracket.
temporal.noteHelp: A verbal description of the temporal information for when an event related to the object's history is estimated to have occurred.
temporal.showDateDetails: Show certainty, qualifier, and additional date information

spatialPlace.showPlaceDetails: Show certainty, coordinates, and additional place information

labels.noteAcquisitionPlace: Additional information about the acquisition place
labels.noteObjectHistoryPlace: Additional information about this place (object history)
labels.noteObjectHistoryEventPlace: Additional information about the event place
labels.noteObjectHistory: Additional information about the object’s history
labels.noteLocation: Additional information about the location record
labels.noteMaterialComponent: Additional information about the material constituent
labels.notePlaceOwnerHistory: Additional information about the ownership place
labels.notePlaceProduction: Additional information about the production place
labels.notePlaceContent: Additional information about the content place
labels.noteExchangeOwnerHistory: Additional information about the ownership transfer
labels.noteLocationSpatial: Additional information about the temporary location
labels.noteObjectHistoryActivity: Description of activity related to the object
labels.name: Name
labels.unit: Unit for the technical attribute measure
labels.eventNameType: Type of event related to the object
labels.usageInstructions: Manner of operation or usage instruction

detail.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete "{{name}}"? This action cannot be undone.

identification.titlesHint: The name given by the artist, maker, or collector at the time the object was created, or later names that are generally understood to refer to the object. A title can be recorded for an artwork, photograph, building, object, moving image, sound recording, book, or document, for example.

acquisition.acquisitionTimeHint: The point in time when ownership or administrative control of the object passed to the museum. It may be the moment of purchase, the date the material was received, or e.g. the date of the acquisition decision.
acquisition.datesHint: Other dates related to the acquisition event—such as the purchase date, transfer date, diary date, or date of the acquisition decision—that cannot be recorded as the acquisition time.
acquisition.placeNameFiLabel: Place name in Finnish (acquisition place information)
acquisition.placeNameEnLabel: Place name in English (acquisition place information)
acquisition.placesHint: The acquisition place and other locations related to the acquisition event, such as the place of transfer and the place of receipt.
acquisition.actorsHint: People, groups of people, or organizations involved in the acquisition event. Examples of acquisition actors include donors, sellers, funders, recipients, intermediaries, those who shaped the acquisition, or those who made the acquisition decision.

history.ownerHistoryHint: Past owners, dates, places, and how ownership changed.
history.productionHint: Who made or produced the object, when, where, and how.
history.productionActorRoleLabel: Role of actor related to production
history.productionDatesHint: Time information related to production. Add one row for each time statement.
history.productionDateRoleLabel: Role of the production time entry
history.productionPlacesHint: Places where the object was designed, created, or produced, or that otherwise relate to production. Add one row per place.
history.productionPlaceNameType: Type of place name related to production
history.productionPlaceRole: Role of place related to production
history.usageHistoryHint: Used when recording information about the use of objects similar to the catalogued object.
history.objectHistoryHint: Used when recording information about matters known to have occurred or to relate to the object’s history.
history.objectHistoryDatesHint: A date or time relating to the object’s history. Record only information that has not already been recorded according to other instructions.
history.objectHistoryDateRoleLabel: Role of time related to the object
history.objectHistoryPlacesHint: Places related to the object’s history. Add one row per place.
history.objectHistoryPlaceNameFi: Place name related to object history (Finnish)
history.objectHistoryPlaceNameEn: Place name related to object history (English)
history.objectHistoryPlaceNameType: Type of place name related to object history
history.objectHistoryPlaceRole: Role of place related to object history
history.associatedEventHint: A historical event related to the object (not production events or events related to collection management). More detailed descriptions of how the event relates to the object are recorded according to the guidance for “Additional information about object history”.
history.eventActorsLegend: Actors in the event related to the object
history.eventDatesLegend: Dates and times for the event related to the object
history.eventDatesHint: A date or time relating to the historical event linked to the object. Record only information that has not already been recorded according to other instructions.
history.eventDateRoleLabel: Role of time related to the event linked to the object
history.eventDateEntryLegend: Time related to the event linked to the object {{n}}
history.eventPlacesLegend: Places for the event related to the object
history.eventPlacesHint: Places related to the historical event linked to the object. Add one row per place.
history.eventPlaceNameFi: Place name related to the event linked to the object (Finnish)
history.eventPlaceNameEn: Place name related to the event linked to the object (English)
history.eventPlaceNameType: Type of place name related to the event linked to the object
history.eventPlaceRole: Role of place related to the event linked to the object

rights.hint:
Used when recording information about rights related to the object. Record information for all rights, regardless of the nature of the right. Objects related to rights are referred to using the object identifier.

For uncertain information and objects without an agreement, record information about the lack of an agreement and incomplete information. Objects that are unclear from a copyright perspective must not be made available for use outside the museum.
rights.holdersHint: Owner of the right related to the object.

access.accessDateLegend: Date when access restriction was recorded
access.statusDateLegend: Date when processing stage was recorded

location.entriesHint: Used when recording information about the object's location on museum premises or elsewhere. See the Location place information section to save descriptive information about the storage space.
location.hint: Place details: name, type, coordinates, owner, and related fields.

description.contentDatesHint: Times represented in or described by the object’s content. Add one row per time expression.
description.contentPlacesHint: Places represented in or described by the object’s content. Add one row per place.

contentActivitySelect.placeholder: Search and select YSO concepts from Finto...

contentKokoSelect.placeholder: Search and pick KOKO concepts from Finto...
contentKokoSelect.loading: Searching the KOKO vocabulary in Finto...

colorAllowlistSelect.placeholder: Select colors or search the list…

identification.owningOrganization: The party that owns the collection. The owner is usually the museum. It may also be a foundation, organization, municipality, or association that owns the museum and manages it.
identification.collection: The collection the object belongs to.
identification.objectKind: Term expressing what kind of object this is.
identification.objectNumber: An identifier for the object. Each object or object group should have a unique identifier, often called an acquisition, diary, inventory, or image number, object number, or file name.
identification.objectName: A term describing the object’s function, form, or type. Add as many names as needed. For example: installation, main category sculptures, special category.
identification.numberOfObjects: How many distinct objects this record refers to.
identification.titleType: The type of title recorded for the object.
identification.titleLanguage: The language in which the object’s foreign-language title is written. Use this when the title language differs from the language the museum generally uses for cataloguing.
identification.noteNimeke: Additional information related to the object’s name. Notes are written as complete sentences with correct grammar. A note may explain the name or reduce ambiguity.

acquisition.referenceNumber: Unique acquisition reference that links to acquisition documentation.

otherTimes.association: Information about how the time relates to an event in the object’s history.
otherTimes.period: The time period during which an event related to the object’s history is estimated to have occurred. It may be, for example, a geological era, historical period, stylistic period, or an art-related period.

latest.date: Latest possible date in this temporal expression.

acquisition.method: Acquisition method expressed as a single term.
acquisition.reason: Acquisition reasons written as complete sentences.
acquisition.acquisitionPlaceRole: Information on how the place relates to the object being catalogued.
acquisition.acquisitionActorRole: The role of the person, group, or organization in the acquisition event. Choose a term from the list below.
acquisition.placeName: Acquisition place. You may record multiple place details.
acquisition.provisos: Restrictions and conditions applied to the acquisition.
acquisition.note: General background information about the acquisition event.
acquisition.groupPurchasePrice: Total price paid for the acquired object group.
acquisition.groupPurchaseDenomination: Currency used for the purchase price.
acquisition.originalPurchasePrice: Price paid for an acquired individual object.

history.ownerActor: Person, group of persons, or organization that owned the object before title passed to the museum.

ownershipDate.note: Record ownership dates as precisely as possible.

history.ownershipPlace: Place where the owner possessed the object.
history.ownershipPlaceRole: Information on how the place relates to the object being catalogued.

productionDate.association: Same closed vocabulary as in other acquisition time fields (role of time): choose a term from the list.

history.productionPlace: Place where the object was designed, created, or produced.
history.productionPlaceNameType: The nature or type of the place.
history.productionPlaceRole: Information on how the place relates to the object being catalogued.
history.productionRelatedActor: Person, group of persons, or organization involved in the object’s design, creation, or production. A production-related actor may also be the commissioner or the party who had the work produced.
history.productionReason: Reason why the object was made.
history.technique: Process, method, or technique used in production.
history.productionTechniqueType: Technique type from the closed list (e.g. art, craft, industrial).

associatedDate.date: A date or time relating to the object’s history.
associatedDate.note: A date or time relating to the object’s history. Record only information that has not already been recorded according to other instructions.
associatedDate.association: Same closed vocabulary as in other acquisition time fields (role of time): choose a term from the list.
associatedDate.period: The period during which an event related to the object’s history is estimated to have occurred — e.g. geological, historical, stylistic, or art-historical period.

associatedEventDate.date: A date or time relating to the historical event linked to the object.
associatedEventDate.note: A date or time relating to the historical event linked to the object. Record only information that has not already been recorded according to other instructions.
associatedEventDate.association: Same closed vocabulary as in other acquisition time fields (role of time): choose a term from the list.
associatedEventDate.period: The period during which the historical event linked to the object is estimated to have occurred — e.g. geological, historical, stylistic, or art-historical period.

history.objectHistoryActivity: An activity that relates to the object’s history.
history.objectHistoryActivityDescription: A verbal description or comments on the activity related to the object.
history.objectHistoryNote: Information about events related to the object’s history, such as manufacture, creation, owners, sellers, circumstances related to events, and other details. Stories and narratives related to the object are also recorded here. Record only information that has not already been recorded according to other instructions.
history.objectHistoryComments: Comments about the object from customers, researchers, or for example collection staff.
history.objectHistoryRelevance: Meanings related to the object and museum value, for example a significance statement produced using a significance analysis method.
history.objectHistoryCulturalAffinity: The broader cultural context to which the object relates.
history.objectHistoryPlaceNameType: The nature or type of the place.
history.objectHistoryPlaceRole: Information on how the place relates to the object being catalogued.
history.objectHistoryEventNameType: The type of the historical event related to the object.
history.objectHistoryActorRole: The role of a person, group of persons, or organization in an object history event. Choose the role from the list below.
history.usagePurpose: A term describing the intended use of objects similar to the catalogued object.
history.usageManner: Describe how objects similar to the catalogued object have been used. Write the description in complete, grammatically correct sentences. Describe the object’s manners of use in different phases of its history. Also state whether the description refers to a specific person or organization, or whether use was widespread and traditional.

rights.entryNote: Additional information about the right related to the object. The additional information may describe the content of the right or specify the nature of the right in more detail. Record only information that has not already been recorded according to other instructions.
rights.type: Nature of the right related to the object.

beginDate.date: Date when the right starts or started.

endDate.date: Date when the right ends or ended.

access.accessCategory: How restricted or unrestricted access to the object is.

accessDate.date: Date when the access restriction was recorded.
accessDate.note: Date when the access restriction was recorded.

access.displayStatus: Processing/display stage of an object prepared for display.

displayStatusDate.date: The date when usage rights were processed or changed.
displayStatusDate.note: The date when usage rights were processed or changed.

access.creditLine: Text naming donor, lender, or owner.
access.note: Additional details about the access category.

location.identifier: Unique location identifier, number, or code.
location.placeNameType: Controlled term for the kind of place name.
location.environmentalDetails: Environmental conditions at or around the place.
location.placeStatus: Special status of the place (e.g. protected area, capital).
location.placeCoordinatesQualifier: Accuracy or precision of the coordinates.
location.coordinateSystem: Coordinate reference system used for the values.
location.placeReferenceNumber: Identifier or code for the place in an external system.
location.placeReferenceNumberType: What kind of reference number this is.
location.placePosition: Free-text description of the place position or location.

date.note: Date when the object was placed in location.

location.fitness: How well location conditions meet object requirements.

confidentiality.note: Restrictions related to confidentiality of object information.
confidentiality.usage: Information on how object data has been used.

description.objectStatus: The catalogued object's position or status in relation to other similar objects.
description.objectComponents: A fixed part or component of the object being described.
description.objectComponentDescription: Verbal description of this part of the object.
description.objectComponentIdentifier: Identifier used for this part or component.
description.materials: Primary materials the object is made of.
description.materialComponents: Name for a significant constituent, addition, or trace related to the object's material—for example information about patina or corrosion that has developed on the object and is considered especially significant and worth documenting.
description.noteMaterialComponent: Clarifying information about the material constituent.
description.materialName: The usual name for the material or a more specific designation of the base material. The name may be colloquial, professional, or general-language usage for a given material type, or a product brand name.
description.materialOrigin: The geographical origin of the material used to make the object, or of the object as found in nature—such as a plant, animal, or geological substance. It may be, for example, the quarry a stone was taken from, the country of origin of timber, or the geological outcrop of imported fossils or rocks. Material origin is not the same as the production-related place, which is where the object is made from the original material.
description.technicalAttributes: Name of a technical attribute related to the object that can be described and defined.
description.valueTechnical: The extent or scalar measure of the object's technical attribute.
description.measurementUnitTechnical: The unit given together with the measure of the technical attribute.
description.inscriptions: Inscriptions on the object are usually described in words, but you should also photograph them and attach the photos with the object’s documentation.
description.copyNumber: Copy number within a limited edition.
description.inscriptionPosition: Part of the object where the inscription is located.
description.inscriptionContent: Textual inscription exactly as it appears on the object.
description.inscriptionDescription: Description of a non-textual marking on the object.
description.inscriptionMethod: Method used to make the inscription.
description.inscriptionDirection: Direction or orientation of the inscription.
description.inscriptionInscriber: The person, group, or organization that made the inscription.
description.interpretationText: Interpretation of an inscription on the object.
description.inscriptionInterpretator: The person or organization that produced the interpretation of the inscription.

inscriptionDate.date: Date when the inscription was made. Record the timing as precisely as possible.
inscriptionDate.note: Date when the inscription was made.

interpretationDate.date: The time when the interpretation of the inscription was made.

contentDate.note: Date represented or described by the object.

description.contentTimeRole: Same closed vocabulary as acquisition “other times” (Time role): pick a Finnish term from the list.
description.contentPlaceRole: Information about how the place relates to the object being catalogued.
description.contentPlaceNameType: The nature or type of the place.
description.contentActivity: Activity depicted in or described by the object.
description.contentSubEventName: Event depicted in or described by the object.
description.contentSubEventNameType: A term that describes the nature of the event related to the content.
description.contentPosition: The part or location in the object where the described information is located. Example: reverse side, edge, front, side 1.
description.contentScript: The writing system used for textual content on the object.
description.contentLanguage: The language in which the object's textual content is written.
description.contentGeneralConcept: Keywords that describe the object's content, context, meaning, appearance, or other properties. Pick terms from the KOKO vocabulary in Finto (http://www.yso.fi/onto/koko/).
description.contentClassification: Classification systems contain classes used to describe object type, meaning, context, appearance, and other properties.
description.contentStyleMao: Styles and schools from the MAO/TAO vocabulary in Finto, rooted at http://www.yso.fi/onto/mao/p178. Pick a listed term; add multiple rows if needed.
description.contentEventName: An event depicted in or described by the object.
description.contentEventType: A term that describes the nature of the event related to the content.
